facts.text: HL7 FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) is the modern healthcare data exchange standard. Core resources: Patient, Encounter, Observation, Condition, MedicationRequest, DiagnosticReport, Procedure, AllergyIntolerance. Uses RESTful API (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), JSON/XML formats, OAuth2 for authorization. SMART on FHIR enables third-party app integration with EHR systems.

ICD-10 (International Classification of Diseases, 10th revision) provides standardized diagnosis codes. Structure: 3-7 characters, first character is letter (chapter), digits 2-3 are category, digit 4+ are specificity. Examples: I21.0 (acute ST-elevation MI of anterior wall), J18.9 (pneumonia, unspecified), E11.65 (type 2 diabetes with hyperglycemia). ICD-10-CM used for diagnosis in US; ICD-10-PCS for inpatient procedures.

CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) codes describe medical procedures and services for billing. Categories: I (procedures/services, 00100-99499), II (performance measures), III (emerging technology). E/M codes (99201-99499) for evaluation and management visits, based on complexity (straightforward, low, moderate, high). Modifiers indicate special circumstances (e.g., -25 significant separate E/M, -59 distinct procedural service).

SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms) is the most comprehensive clinical terminology with 350,000+ concepts. Organized in hierarchies: Clinical Finding, Procedure, Body Structure, Organism, Substance, Pharmaceutical Product. Each concept has a unique SCTID, descriptions (preferred/synonym), and relationships (is-a, finding site, causative agent). Used for clinical documentation, decision support, and analytics.

DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) is the standard for medical imaging. Stores images with metadata (patient demographics, study/series/instance UIDs, modality, acquisition parameters). Modalities: CT, MRI, US (ultrasound), XR (X-ray), MG (mammography), NM (nuclear medicine), PT (PET). PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System) stores and distributes DICOM images. Transfer syntaxes define compression (JPEG, JPEG2000, RLE).
